course_description.overview: Serbia's flagship mass-participation road cycling event starting in Belgrade, looping through urban areas and ascending Avala mountain with rolling terrain and key climbs.

key_segments.name: Avala Mountain Climb
key_segments.description: Signature ascent on Avala with steady gradients leading to panoramic views.

key_segments.name: Belgrade City Loop
key_segments.description: Flat to rolling urban and suburban roads through historic Belgrade.

key_segments.name: Return to Belgrade
key_segments.description: Mixed terrain with final rollers approaching the finish.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Moderate difficulty for gran fondos: demanding due to Avala climbs and total distance, accessible for trained amateurs with time limit.

logistics.getting_there: Fly into Belgrade Nikola Tesla Airport (BEG), 30-min drive to city center; event start in central Belgrade.
logistics.accommodation: Abundant hotels in Belgrade city center; book early for race weekend near start/finish.
logistics.registration_details: Online via official site; early bird fees ~€50-80; UCI Gran Fondo qualifier slots available.
logistics.bike_logistics: Bike rental shops in Belgrade; fly with bike or ship; mechanics on-site pre-race.
logistics.local_considerations: Warm June weather (20-30°C); Balkan roads in good condition; English widely spoken in city.

final_verdict.one_liner: Belgrade's premier gran fondo blending city vibes, mountain challenge, and Balkan hospitality.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Amateur road cyclists seeking a scenic European qualifier with moderate difficulty and great value.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for accessible long-distance road riding in an emerging cycling nation.
pro_tips: Train for Avala climbs with hill repeats.
